comment_text.sn12.4:1.2: In early texts, Vipassī is invariably mentioned along with the full group of seven Buddhas (Thag 7.5, DN 32:3.2, Bu Pj 1:3.1.6). Their details are given in the Mahāpadāna Sutta (DN 14). There, the story of Vipassī is recounted in full; the key doctrinal passages are shared with the current discourse (DN 14:2.18.5). | Bodhisatta is explained by the commentary here as either “one possessed of awakening”, or one who is certain of awakening in the proximity of Buddhahood. The latter explanation allows a reading equivalent to Sanskrit bodhiśakta (“one intent on awakening”), rather than the more familiar bodhisattva (“awakening-being”).
comment_text.sn12.4:2.2: This phrasing corresponds to the abstract principle of dependent origination (SN 12.37:2.2). Compare Śaunaka’s question at Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad 1.2.3:  “When what is understood, sir, is all this understood?” (kasmin nu bhagavo vijñāte sarvaṁ idaṁ vijñātaṁ bhavati).
comment_text.sn12.4:2.3: Yoniso maniskāra (“rational application of mind”) is a distinctively Buddhist term that literally means “applying the mind by way of source”. It is mostly used in investigating causality, although over time it came to have a more general sense of “reflection, inquiry, attention”.
comment_text.sn12.4:11.4: Here we have the normal twelve links, whereas in DN 14 the sequence omits ignorance and choices, emphasizing rather the mutual interdependence of consciousness with name and form, a variation found also in the context of Gotama’s awakening at SN 12.65.
comment_text.sn12.4:25.4: This phrasing recalls the Buddha’s first sermon (SN 56.11:5.1), an insight that is said to be common to all Buddhas (SN 56.12:1.1). | Pubbe ananussutesu (“not learned before from another”) means that this is a fresh insight not passed down in an oral tradition.
comment_text.sn12.4:26.1: This instruction is noted in the Pali text.

root_text.sn12.4:1.2: “vipassissa, bhikkhave, bhagavato arahato sammāsambuddhassa pubbeva sambodhā anabhisambuddhassa bodhisattasseva sato etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:1.3: ‘kicchaṁ vatāyaṁ loko āpanno jāyati ca jīyati ca mīyati ca cavati ca upapajjati ca.
root_text.sn12.4:1.4: Atha ca panimassa dukkhassa nissaraṇaṁ nappajānāti jarāmaraṇassa.
root_text.sn12.4:1.5: Kudāssu nāma imassa dukkhassa nissaraṇaṁ paññāyissati jarāmaraṇassā’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:2.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:2.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati jarāmaraṇaṁ hoti, kiṁpaccayā jarāmaraṇan’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:2.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:2.4: ‘jātiyā kho sati jarāmaraṇaṁ hoti, jātipaccayā jarāmaraṇan’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:3.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:3.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati jāti hoti, kiṁpaccayā jātī’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:3.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:3.4: ‘bhave kho sati jāti hoti, bhavapaccayā jātī’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:4.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:4.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati bhavo hoti, kiṁpaccayā bhavo’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:4.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:4.4: ‘upādāne kho sati bhavo hoti, upādānapaccayā bhavo’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:5.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:5.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati upādānaṁ hoti, kiṁpaccayā upādānan’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:5.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:5.4: ‘taṇhāya kho sati upādānaṁ hoti, taṇhāpaccayā upādānan’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:6.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:6.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati taṇhā hoti, kiṁpaccayā taṇhā’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:6.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:6.4: ‘vedanāya kho sati taṇhā hoti, vedanāpaccayā taṇhā’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:7.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:7.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati vedanā hoti, kiṁpaccayā vedanā’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:7.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:7.4: ‘phasse kho sati vedanā hoti, phassapaccayā vedanā’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:8.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:8.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati phasso hoti, kiṁpaccayā phasso’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:8.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:8.4: ‘saḷāyatane kho sati phasso hoti, saḷāyatanapaccayā phasso’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:9.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:9.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati saḷāyatanaṁ hoti, kiṁpaccayā saḷāyatanan’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:9.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:9.4: ‘nāmarūpe kho sati saḷāyatanaṁ hoti, nāmarūpapaccayā saḷāyatanan’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:10.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:10.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati nāmarūpaṁ hoti, kiṁpaccayā nāmarūpan’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:10.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:10.4: ‘viññāṇe kho sati nāmarūpaṁ hoti, viññāṇapaccayā nāmarūpan’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:11.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:11.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati viññāṇaṁ hoti, kiṁpaccayā viññāṇan’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:11.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:11.4: ‘saṅkhāresu kho sati viññāṇaṁ hoti, saṅkhārapaccayā viññāṇan’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:12.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:12.2: ‘kimhi nu kho sati saṅkhārā honti, kiṁpaccayā saṅkhārā’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:12.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:12.4: ‘avijjāya kho sati saṅkhārā honti, avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārā’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:13.4: ‘Samudayo, samudayo’ti kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn12.4:14.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:14.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati jarāmaraṇaṁ na hoti, kissa nirodhā jarāmaraṇanirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:14.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:14.4: ‘jātiyā kho asati jarāmaraṇaṁ na hoti, jātinirodhā jarāmaraṇanirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:15.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:15.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati jāti na hoti, kissa nirodhā jātinirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:15.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:15.4: ‘bhave kho asati jāti na hoti, bhavanirodhā jātinirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:16.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:16.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati bhavo na hoti, kissa nirodhā bhavanirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:16.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:16.4: ‘upādāne kho asati bhavo na hoti, upādānanirodhā bhavanirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:17.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:17.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati upādānaṁ na hoti, kissa nirodhā upādānanirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:17.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:17.4: ‘taṇhāya kho asati upādānaṁ na hoti, taṇhānirodhā upādānanirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:18.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:18.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati taṇhā na hoti, kissa nirodhā taṇhānirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:18.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:18.4: ‘vedanāya kho asati taṇhā na hoti, vedanānirodhā taṇhānirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:19.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:19.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati vedanā na hoti, kissa nirodhā vedanānirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:19.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:19.4: ‘phasse kho asati vedanā na hoti, phassanirodhā vedanānirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:20.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:20.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati phasso na hoti, kissa nirodhā phassanirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:20.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:20.4: ‘saḷāyatane kho asati phasso na hoti, saḷāyatananirodhā phassanirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:21.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:21.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati saḷāyatanaṁ na hoti, kissa nirodhā saḷāyatananirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:21.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:21.4: ‘nāmarūpe kho asati saḷāyatanaṁ na hoti, nāmarūpanirodhā saḷāyatananirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:22.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:22.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati nāmarūpaṁ na hoti, kissa nirodhā nāmarūpanirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:22.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:22.4: ‘viññāṇe kho asati nāmarūpaṁ na hoti, viññāṇanirodhā nāmarūpanirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:23.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:23.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati viññāṇaṁ na hoti, kissa nirodhā viññāṇanirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:23.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:23.4: ‘saṅkhāresu kho asati viññāṇaṁ na hoti, saṅkhāranirodhā viññāṇanirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:24.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa etadahosi:
root_text.sn12.4:24.2: ‘kimhi nu kho asati saṅkhārā na honti, kissa nirodhā saṅkhāranirodho’ti?
root_text.sn12.4:24.3: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa yoniso manasikārā ahu paññāya abhisamayo:
root_text.sn12.4:24.4: ‘avijjāya kho asati saṅkhārā na honti, avijjānirodhā saṅkhāranirodho’ti.
root_text.sn12.4:25.4: ‘Nirodho, nirodho’ti kho, bhikkhave, vipassissa bodhisattassa pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.4:1.2: “Mendicants, Vipassī the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha had this thought before his awakening, when he was still unawakened but intent on awakening:
translation_text.sn12.4:1.3: ‘Alas, this world has fallen into trouble. It’s born, grows old, dies, passes away, and is reborn,
translation_text.sn12.4:1.4: yet it doesn’t understand how to escape from this suffering, from old age and death.
translation_text.sn12.4:1.5: Oh, when will an escape be found from this suffering, from old age and death?’
translation_text.sn12.4:2.1: Then Vipassī, the one intent on awakening, thought:
translation_text.sn12.4:2.2: ‘When what exists is there old age and death? What is a requirement for old age and death?’
translation_text.sn12.4:2.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:2.4: ‘When rebirth exists there’s old age and death. Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death.’
translation_text.sn12.4:3.2: ‘When what exists is there rebirth? What is a requirement for rebirth?’
translation_text.sn12.4:3.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:3.4: ‘When continued existence exists there’s rebirth. Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.’
translation_text.sn12.4:4.2: ‘When what exists is there continued existence? What is a requirement for continued existence?’
translation_text.sn12.4:4.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:4.4: ‘When grasping exists there’s continued existence. Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.’
translation_text.sn12.4:5.2: ‘When what exists is there grasping? What is a requirement for grasping?’
translation_text.sn12.4:5.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:5.4: ‘When craving exists there’s grasping. Craving is a requirement for grasping.’
translation_text.sn12.4:6.2: ‘When what exists is there craving? What is a requirement for craving?’
translation_text.sn12.4:6.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:6.4: ‘When feeling exists there’s craving. Feeling is a requirement for craving.’
translation_text.sn12.4:7.2: ‘When what exists is there feeling? What is a requirement for feeling?’
translation_text.sn12.4:7.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:7.4: ‘When contact exists there’s feeling. Contact is a requirement for feeling.’
translation_text.sn12.4:8.2: ‘When what exists is there contact? What is a requirement for contact?’
translation_text.sn12.4:8.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:8.4: ‘When the six sense fields exist there’s contact. The six sense fields are requirements for contact.’
translation_text.sn12.4:9.2: ‘When what exists are there the six sense fields? What is a requirement for the six sense fields?’
translation_text.sn12.4:9.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:9.4: ‘When name and form exist there are the six sense fields. Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields.’
translation_text.sn12.4:10.2: ‘When what exists are there name and form? What is a requirement for name and form?’
translation_text.sn12.4:10.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:10.4: ‘When consciousness exists there are name and form. Consciousness is a requirement for name and form.’
translation_text.sn12.4:11.2: ‘When what exists is there consciousness? What is a requirement for consciousness?’
translation_text.sn12.4:11.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:11.4: ‘When choices exist there’s consciousness. Choices are a requirement for consciousness.’
translation_text.sn12.4:12.2: ‘When what exists are there choices? What is a requirement for choices?’
translation_text.sn12.4:12.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:12.4: ‘When ignorance exists there are choices. Ignorance is a requirement for choices.’
translation_text.sn12.4:13.1: And so, ignorance is a requirement for choices.
translation_text.sn12.4:13.2: Choices are a requirement for consciousness. …
translation_text.sn12.4:13.3: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.4:13.4: ‘Origination, origination.’ While Vipassī was intent on awakening, such was the vision, knowledge, wisdom, realization, and light that arose in him regarding teachings not learned before from another.
translation_text.sn12.4:14.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no old age and death? When what ceases do old age and death cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:14.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:14.4: ‘When rebirth doesn’t exist there’s no old age and death. When rebirth ceases, old age and death cease.’
translation_text.sn12.4:15.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no rebirth? When what ceases does rebirth cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:15.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:15.4: ‘When continued existence doesn’t exist there’s no rebirth. When continued existence ceases, rebirth ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:16.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no continued existence? When what ceases does continued existence cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:16.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:16.4: ‘When grasping doesn’t exist there’s no continued existence. When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:17.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no grasping? When what ceases does grasping cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:17.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:17.4: ‘When craving doesn’t exist there’s no grasping. When craving ceases, grasping ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:18.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no craving? When what ceases does craving cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:18.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:18.4: ‘When feeling doesn’t exist there’s no craving. When feeling ceases, craving ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:19.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no feeling? When what ceases does feeling cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:19.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:19.4: ‘When contact doesn’t exist there’s no feeling. When contact ceases, feeling ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:20.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no contact? When what ceases does contact cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:20.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:20.4: ‘When the six sense fields don’t exist there’s no contact. When the six sense fields cease, contact ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:21.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist are there no six sense fields? When what ceases do the six sense fields cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:21.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:21.4: ‘When name and form don’t exist there are no six sense fields. When name and form cease, the six sense fields cease.’
translation_text.sn12.4:22.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist are there no name and form? When what ceases do name and form cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:22.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:22.4: ‘When consciousness doesn’t exist there are no name and form. When consciousness ceases, name and form cease.’
translation_text.sn12.4:23.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist is there no consciousness? When what ceases does consciousness cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:23.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:23.4: ‘When choices don’t exist there’s no consciousness. When choices cease, consciousness ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.4:24.2: ‘When what doesn’t exist are there no choices? When what ceases do choices cease?’
translation_text.sn12.4:24.3: Then, through rational application of mind, Vipassī penetrated with wisdom:
translation_text.sn12.4:24.4: ‘When ignorance doesn’t exist there are no choices. When ignorance ceases, choices cease.’
translation_text.sn12.4:25.1: And so, when ignorance ceases, choices cease.
translation_text.sn12.4:25.3: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.
translation_text.sn12.4:25.4: ‘Cessation, cessation.’ Such was the vision, knowledge, wisdom, realization, and light that arose in Vipassī, the one intent on awakening, regarding teachings not learned before from another.”
translation_text.sn12.4:26.1: (Tell in full  for each of the seven Buddhas.)
